messages.content:
[Response must be written as a short blurb.]
Why can't I remember my dreams?

Many people don't remember their dreams due to the natural cycle of sleep, particularly the fluctuation of brain activity and the phases we go through, like REM (Rapid Eye Movement) sleep, where dreaming mainly occurs. Factors like not waking up during or right after REM sleep, the role of neurotransmitters, or simply not paying enough attention to dream experiences can contribute to forgetfulness. Additionally, stress, lack of sleep, and various external influences can also affect dream recall. If you're keen on remembering your dreams, consider keeping a dream journal and writing down any fragments as soon as you wake up.

[Response must be written as a short blurb.]
Is it true that it is dangerous to awaken a sleepwalker?

Waking a sleepwalker is not typically dangerous for them, but it may cause confusion or disorientation. It's generally safer to gently guide them back to bed. The idea that waking a sleepwalker is hazardous is largely a myth, but caution is advised to prevent injury if they react abruptly.
What administrative division was Jerusalem in under the Ottomans in 1914?

In 1914, under Ottoman rule, Jerusalem was part of the vilayet (province) of Syria, specifically within the sanjak (district) of Jerusalem. The Ottoman administrative divisions organized regions into a hierarchical structure of vilayets, sanjaks, and kazas.
Did the Parthians ever control Jerusalem?

Yes, the Parthians did briefly control Jerusalem. In 40 BCE, the Parthian Empire conquered the city as part of their incursions into the region, installing Antigonus II Mattathias as the king of Judea. Their control was short-lived, as the Romans led by Herod the Great recaptured Jerusalem in 37 BCE.
What administrative division was Jerusalem in under the Ottomans in 1862?

In 1862, Jerusalem was part of the Ottoman Empire's administrative division known as the Eyalet of Damascus. The city was not yet a separate administrative unit; it became its own sanjak (district), within the Vilayet of Syria, only later in Ottoman administrative reforms.
What administrative division was Jerusalem in under the Ottomans in 1744?

In 1744, Jerusalem was part of the Damascus Eyalet of the Ottoman Empire. This administrative structure was composed of several sanjaks or districts, but the significant administrative reforms, known as the Tanzimat, which would later reorganize the empire’s territories into vilayets and more modern structures, were not implemented until the mid-19th century.
What administrative division was Beirut in under the Ottomans in 1914?

In 1914, Beirut was the administrative center of its own vilayet, the Vilayet of Beirut, within the Ottoman Empire. It had been established as a separate province in 1888 due to its economic importance as a major port city on the Mediterranean coast.

In 1914, Baghdad was under the control of the Ottoman Empire, serving as the administrative center of the Baghdad Vilayet (Province). The Ottoman Turks maintained control over the region until the end of World War I, when British forces took over as part of the Mesopotamian campaign.

In 1744, Baghdad was under the control of the Mamluk governors, within the administrative framework of the Ottoman Empire. These Mamluk rulers were autonomous in many respects but officially acknowledged Ottoman sovereignty. This Mamluk regime in Baghdad would last until the early 19th century when the Ottomans reasserted direct control over the region.
